title: Marmaroxylon claviflorum
intro: Marmaroxylon claviflorum, also known as the "Guayaquil ebony," is a tropical tree native to South America, belonging to the family Bignoniaceae. The wood and bark of Marmaroxylon claviflorum have been traditionally used in folk medicine, and studies have shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Marmaroxylon claviflorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Marmaroxylon claviflorum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of various herbal supplements and remedies.
